2026-04-22T21:13:15.9443819Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T21:13:15.9444325Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T21:13:15.9444720Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T21:13:15.9445067Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T21:13:15.9445356Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T21:13:15.9445598Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T21:13:15.9445755Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T21:13:15.9445930Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T21:13:15.9446326Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-22T21:13:15.9446578Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-22T21:13:15.9446777Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T21:13:15.9447047Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T21:13:15.9447295Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

The explicit `` (resolved to CPM\\u0026#39;s 1.21+ facade) in these test projects acts as a deliberate pin that bumps the transitive 1.17.1 → 1.21+, avoiding the CS0433 \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;type exists in both\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34; conflict with `Azure.Core 1.53`.\\n\\nThe local `sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/src` is already at `1.14.0-beta.1` and no longer references `Azure.Identity` — once that version (or a 1.14 GA) is published to NuGet and the CPM is bumped, the explicit pins in these two test projects can be removed.\\n\\n**Cleanup steps once `Microsoft.Extensions.Azure 1.14` ships:**\\n1. Bump the central `PackageVersion` for `Microsoft.Extensions.Azure` in `eng/centralpackagemanagement/Directory.Packages.props`.\\n2. Remove the explicit `` from the two test projects above.\\n3. The explicit `` (resolved to CPM\\u0026#39;s 1.21+ facade) in these test projects acts as a deliberate pin that bumps the transitive 1.17.1 → 1.21+, avoiding the CS0433 \\u0026#34;\\u0026#34;type exists in both\\u0026#34;\\u0026#34; conflict with `Azure.Core 1.53`.\\n\\nThe local `sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/src` is already at `1.14.0-beta.1` and no longer references `Azure.Identity` — once that version (or a 1.14 GA) is published to NuGet and the CPM is bumped, the explicit pins in these two test projects can be removed.\\n\\n**Cleanup steps once `Microsoft.Extensions.Azure 1.14` ships:**\\n1.
